Discussion in 'Alternative Theories' started by amber, Feb 1, 2018.
(surface's max gravity falls off in both directions, inward and out) see topic, "where is more gravity, inside or out?".
better build your tall buildings near the Equator, no speed effects on the poles, just a nice twist., this.
thank you, Amber, we are having a great time with this.
Found a piece of amber once on the beach with an enclosed insect, it's body having defied increase in entropy for the longest time.
I am voting with you, time is fundamental.
flight through time without formulae.
How do you know what he is thinking?
That is what I did not say.
You may be thinking about weightlessnes a condition that depends in part upon gravity.
But did you weigh either of them?
A Satellite is not weightless , it still experiences the force of gravity and follows a curvature path because of this.
If it were not for the force of gravity and Newtons of weight , the Satellite would travel a straight path and travel away from the Earth.
In a free fall situation a person aboard a falling craft will fall with the craft and seem weightless.
I vaguely remember seeing something once, where aeroplanes go up and down to simulate the affects of weightless.
A person or object could only be weightless if they were at a position in the Universe where forces did not act.
I have a time dilation question for you kind people.
In the Caesium atom experiment , if a person(s) could count out aloud, fast enough to count the frequency of the caesium atom, when one person is counting slower , does this not just mean they are counting slower?
Yes they would most certainly be out of synchronisation.
But would counting slow affect time?
It would ruin the song thats for sure.
If you are counting the intervals slow thats ok so long as you keep up I guess but ...
I may as well type slow for slow readers..neither makes sense.
Try some digital scales by placeing them upside down on the ground that should do it.
How did you see the read out?
The scales would then be measuring their own weight.
A satellite maintains its orbit by balancing two factors: its velocity (the speed it takes to travel in a straight line) and the gravitational pull that Earth has on it.
A satellite orbiting closer to the Earth requires more velocity to resist the stronger gravitational pull.
If gravity did not have a hold on the satellite it would fly off into space and not follow a curvature path.
The speed stops it falling to Earth and gravity stops if flying off into space.
I do not think you could have such a box, but if you had a box that could stop loss and gain of energy then I assume time would stop for the interior of the box.


I think it would be similar to Antoine-Laurent de Lavoisier's work and that nothing is ever lost or gained, but in the scenario of a special box, it includes photon energy.
You did not read it how I thought I had wrote it if you can follow that.
So you have decided - arbitrarily - that a change in entropy is what "causes" time (with no evidence, no supporting argument) and therefore you're arguing the conclusion from that premise.
IOW a circular argument based on an unsupported premise.
You say no, counting slow would not affect time, yet we say when the detector in the Caesium clock counts slow, it affects time, why?

original text:
"O să vină ea aici."
why is the ea not before the verb?
The word order is usually more flexible in Romanian, and, along with intonation, plays a role in setting the emphasis of the message.
Let's play around with this example, shall we?
"Ea o să vină aici."
- standard neutral form, no emphasis, take it how you want it.
"
- specifies that SHE is the one who will come HERE, instead of (for example) HIM going over THERE.
This was a (not exhaustive) list of variants that make sense to my ear.

The Nokia 5300 is a music enabled mobile phone that promises captivating music and more to end-users.
On first glance, one would like the way the handset looks.
This sophisticated gadget comes in a compact exterior, a sleek slide open design and makes a positive impact on an onlooker.
The music options of the Nokia 5300 are quite varied and equally interesting.
With the Nokia 5300, a world of fun and music is waiting to be explored.
There in an integrated multimedia player, which can play music files in formats as diverse as MP3, Midi, AAC to AAC+, enhanced AAC+, and WMA.
There are large and tactile keys to control the multimedia player � so it becomes quite easy for a person to enjoy his favourite music tracks, whenever and wherever he wants to.
The sound output is quite good; and with dedicated volume keys � it is possible to personalize the listening experience to a large extent.
A 1.3-megapixel camera with 8x digital zoom is another key feature of this versatile handset.
The camera can be used to shoot photos in sequence mode; the best frames among those taken can then be selected and shared with family and friends.
Bluetooth compatibility of the Nokia 5300 ensures easy transfer of the images and video clips to personal computers and printers.
As an owner of this amazing mobile phone, you are free to enjoy the sounds of the latest ringtones.
You could, for instance, download your choice of mp3 ringtones, video ringtones, midi ringtones or true tones and give a definite identity to your handset.
With up to 5MB of internal user memory, which you can enhance depending upon your specific needs, there is no dearth of storage space for all the music files, images, video clips, ringtones and other content.
Tri-band connectivity ensures that you remain connected in diverse areas of the globe.
About the Author: Keith Rickwood is webmaster of .
This website deals in contract mobile phones and sim free phones on major six mobile networks in UK.
You Can find best mobile phone deals with latest handset and cheap mobile phone plans on popular mobile networks.

original text:
Psychedelics, neuroscience, and historical biography come together when a journalist finds a lost photograph of Aldous Huxley and uncovers a hidden side of the celebrated author of Brave New World and The Doors of Perception.
Allene Symons had no inkling that Aldous Huxley was once a friend of her father's until the summer of 2001 when she discovered a box of her dad's old photographs.
For years in the 1940s and '50s, her father had meticulously photographed human hands in the hope of developing a science of predicting human aptitudes and even mental illness.
In the box, along with all the other hand images, was one with the name of Aldous Huxley on the back.
How was it possible for two such unlikely people to cross paths--her aircraft-engineer father and the famous author?
This question sparked a journalist's quest to understand what clearly seemed to be a little-known interest of Aldous Huxley.
Through interviews, road trips, and family documents, the author reconstructs a time peaking in mid-1950s Los Angeles when Huxley experimented with psychedelic substances, ran afoul of gatekeepers, and advocated responsible use of such hallucinogens to treat mental illness as well as to achieve states of mind called mystical.
Because the author's father had studied hundreds of hands, including those of schizophrenics, he was invited into Huxley's research and discussion circle.
This intriguing narrative about the early psychedelic era throws new light on one of the 20th-century's foremost intellectuals, showing that his experiments in consciousness presaged pivotal scientific research underway today.
Allene Symons is the author of Nostradamus, Vagabond Prophet: A Novel of His Life and Time and an instructor of communications and media studies at Santa Ana College.
She previously worked as a senior editor at Publishers Weekly, Drug Store News, and For the Pharmacist magazines and as general manager of Rizzoli International Bookstore in Costa Mesa, California.

original text:
Pine Sport was created to give athletes, riders & coaches somewhere to come to receive quality coaching & mentoring either as individuals or as groups.
We have years of experience in working at a high level in MTB, Road, Time Trial & track cycling to give a broad spectrum of services, these include in addition to coaching; Coach Mentoring & MTB Guiding by qualified British Cycling MTB Leaders.
We are also UKAD Accredited Anti-Doping Advisors and Educators to ensure that you get the most up to date advice & knowledge.
Pine Sport will be working with Evans Cycles as part of their community project to deliver weekly turbo sessions in store at Guildford along with new store rides that will take place fortnightly.
Pine Sport have teamed up with Eventrex, the guys that run the hugely popular Southern XC Series to run coaching and course practice sessions at all of the series races in 2019.
For more details, click on the pic!
We don't just go off road, watch out for road and track sessions too!

original text:
Maxcine joined Saracens in June 2018.
She is an Associate of the Chartered Institute of Legal Executives ("CILEX") and has also completed the Prince 2 in Management and Leadership.
Maxcine studied CILEX at the University of Westminster.
She has extensive experience of over 15 years in residential and commercial property conveyancing, as well as wills and probate.
Her main expertise is within residential conveyancing.
Over the course of her career, Maxcine has worked in the public, charity and private sectors.
Outside the office, Maxcine enjoys spending time with her family and friends, who she describes as her heartbeat.
She works with a team in the Women's Department at her local church, providing support, seminars, retreats and other activities.
Maxcine enjoys listening to gospel music, attending the theatre; watching political and current affairs programmes.
Her favourite authors are Jane Austen and John Grisham.
